question.text: Rani has existing equity mutual fund investments worth Rs.10,00,000 expected to grow at 12% annually. What will this investment be worth in 25 years?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Future Value = Rs.10,00,000 x (1.12)^25 = Rs.10,00,000 x 17.0001 = Rs.1,70,00,064 approximately. **The Trap:** Some may try to use simple interest. Always use compound interest for long-term projections. **Cross-Exam Context:** Future value calculations for existing investments are essential when computing the gap in retirement planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always account for existing investments before computing the fresh savings needed.
